Full_Review: The ambiance with the view was excellent from the terrace

Didn t like the fact that the windows were sealed so no fresh air and air con was a bit too hot for us   Great location near to Tube station for all of central London attractions and Kensington High Street so no need to pay for expensive food in hotel Clean and comfortable room Easy reach from Paddington and our cases were kept safe in the hotel on the last day so we could explore more of London before returning home

They were unable to process our Amex Card Yet we had no trouble using it anywhere else in London  Great position walk to Kensington Palace Hyde Park close to station buses Helpful staff great amenities comfortable good size rooms

Was a little pricey and bed was not as comfortable as I had expected Two lifts were in need of repair and so avoided them Pleasant enough no WOW factor   The position and restaurant selections were great and the club lounge provided a nice space for happy hour or just a quiet space during the day Staff were good and the stay was trouble free

Need to work on the lifts elevators they weren t working correctly the entire week I even stepped out of an elevator twice because of alarm bells and malfunctions m  Your staff is great The location of the property although a bit off the beaten path iit is the best combination of quiet and near enough An easy walk to a main street underground station and bustling activity

Room keys did not work we were given the wrong room number Two of the lights did not work The hairdryer did not work Unhelpful and unpleasant staff no apology given for inconvenience caused Will not book this hotel again

Room was very dark had a job to see no main lighting bed lamp didn t work but managed with the other one till that one blew Carpet by bathroom was wet and stained think there must have been a leak but all mentioned to staff to look into   Nice to be upgraded to club class Great breakfast great view from bedroom and very comfortable Lovely Hotel very friendly staff and would definately stay here again

The check in is very slow and the lifts are a nightmare to use as they don t make it clear to people that they need to use their room key You often see the same people just going up and down in very full lifts not able to figure out why the lift hasn t stopped on their floor   the location is good

The bed was quite old stained It was reasonably comfortable though A little TLC in the rooms would be beneficial glue up the peeling wallpaper etc  The location was great everything was easily accessible The rooms were large light Check in out were very easy

Right beside railway but noise not too bad Breakfast a bit underwhelming for 4 star hotel no veggie sausages for my veggie partner Cold croissants etc   Staff v friendly and helpful great location just off kensington high street Facilties and room v good Fridge in room great extra

We had booked 3 rooms one was for my disabled elderly mother in law and her carer The hotel did not have the correct number of beds in the rooms They kept us waiting for 0ver 3 hours trying to organise extra beds which never appeared When we eventually got beds for everyone the rooms reeked of cigarette smoke Some of the sausages at breakfast were half raw Perhaps they were trying to poison us It was a complete shambles None of the staff seemed to have a clue and mostly spoke poor English After 2 weeks we eventually received an apology and refund for one of the 3 rooms Would never stay here again under any circumstances

Reception was poorly managed some of the lifts were not working properly There were not adequate towels placed in the room and we had to call a number of times before we got any After 1 night they again didn t sort the towels out and did not replenish the tea coffee until asked to There was also not enough toilet roll and we also had to ask for some more We complained to reception but they didn t seem to care but eventually offered to have the manager call us I still haven t heard from them
We booked a club room but it was just an ordinary room Poor quality toiletries no robes as advertised etc  this hotel is well located near Kensington High Street

Due to an event I was attending I contacted the hotel to enquire if I could drop my luggage off before room checkin I was informed I could do this The staff were very helpful on doing this When I did arrive to check in which was late at night the service was very prompt

We had a very poor night s sleep because the mattress was far too firm and the synthetic pillows were hard We made a verbal complaint at reception and all they offered to do was to inform the management The lifts were operated via the room s swipe card but were temperamental stopping unexpectedly between floors on all but one journey we made We talked to fellow customers who said they had experienced the same problem We didn t report this because we had already complained about the bed Not surprisingly we won t be staying at the hotel again   The location was good Very close to the tube bus and nice shops
I didn t like having to come home   I stay here every time I am in London The location facilities and above all the staff are so professional and helpful

I have no complaint about this property   Very close to public transportation restaurants stores and to tourist attractions The room was comfortable and a good sized The staff were exceptionally helpful I would definitely go back in the future
club lounge breakfast cold brassiere also no cookies or muffins just plain biscuits no afternoon tea Service in the club lounge in the morning a shamble Staff at reception not very helpful

Breakfast very crowded and slightly chaotic Coffee was lukewarm and seemed to be served whenever it suited the waiters rather than the customer No hash browns or other form of potato and no omelette fresh egg station which has now become common in most hotels At 13 50 per person breakfast was poor value and there was no light alternative breakfast without leaving the hotel   Room was clean and comfortable Hotel is well located being close to the Underground and within walking distance of Royal Albert Hall Kensington Palace etc

The bath room flooded when we showered The maintenance man who came to fix it was surly and grumpy He used all the towels to mop the water and left them in a soggy heap on the bathroom floor The radiator leaked we were woken by the drip drip I used a glass and our towels to soak up the water   I liked the breakfasts the efficiency of the reception staff and the location The conference I attended was in a well ventilated room Lunch involved long queues which took out a lot of the lunch time Could have been better explained and organised

Slight frustration that there were no towels available on our arrival and we were not able to shower before going out in the evening after travelling all day staff did apologise but should not happen really   The location is very good

There were a few problems On arriving in the room found it was made up for a twin but we booked 9 double rooms so we had to ask to move There was only 1 towel in the room We had to ask 3 times to get an extra towel which finally arrived at gone midnight Twice during the evening and next morning we had to get the key reset for the room as it did not work This was annoying after getting to the room and having to go all the way back to reception   This is the 6th time of staying at the hotel The location is fantastic and very convenient
I m not a fan of carpeting   The hotel is not the prettiest on the outside but the room was clean and staff was friendly and helpful Only a few minute s walk from High Street Kensington tube station
Elevator  Strategic location many eateries and shopping outlets nearby ATM machine in the ground floor so i don t need to find currency exchange outlets

Found the room very hot stuffy at night even if we did have the aircon on full bore all day everyday and it was cold outside Had to sleep with the curtains open becoz of the way the aircon faces so early starts every morning   Taxi waiting outside mostdays or 100m round the corner close to tube
Television didn t work very well and a booked wake up call wasn t made  The location is excellent parking is very convenient Room was comfortable and quiet

The pillow is quite flat only one bathrobe and 1 pair of bedroom slipper provided The breakfast spread remains the same for the 5 days that i was there   The location is great with shops restaurants cafes nearby The lounge room and lounge floor makes me feel more secure as I wa travelling alone with my 10yr old

Bit chaotic with poor communication Room not fully ready although we d spoken to them about early check in and they d confirmed okay But when we came back several hours later they said no room was ready and to come back in an hour or pay to upgrade to a more expensive room Finally they got us in a room that wasn t really ready bed configuration was wrong so someone had to come and rearrange the room shower gel and other bathroom items not fully stocked so we had to call down for those But they did sort these later when we asked Just took about an hour to get room sorted Content and decor in room and bathroom a little old and worn but clean and sufficient and comfortable   Staff friendly and tried to be helpful but was a little chaotic Place was clean and correct rooms good and comfortable

No breakfast The lift were not very good Breakfast not included and yet price of room 110 per night and that was supposed to be a bargain   There was terrible noise all morning on my last day How could they organise for loud drilling work to be done when there are gusts next door The lack of breakfast included was a bit tight considering the cost of the room

The place was a lot older than I thought The mugs were sometimes not cleaned and the carpet was somewhat old and dusty A little bit of renovation work would really make this place better   Regarding the other hotels nearby this hotel has bigger rooms considering the price The High Street Kensington station is only 3 min walk away and the underground tube easily takes you to the main spots in London in 15 20 mins

The bed was just a double bed normally I have found hotels have king size Our friends who traveled with us found the double size too small My husband and I would have preferred king size too The bed was comfortable and we slept well enough though The furniture was a bit dated and could probably do with an upgrade but the room was very clean and a good size   The breakfast choice and quality was very good All the staff were very friendly and helpful We actually had an excellent view over the city from our room as we were on the 12th floor However our friends who travelled with us were less fortunate as they were allocated the 5th floor

We were given a disabled room and the automatic door was heavy and i was standing in the open door trying to take room service off the waiter when the door hit me really hard and nearly dropped the room service
I will not come back to this hotel It s old looks great on pictures but reality is disappointing   Location is very good at Kensington with underground connection and a lot of buses

Concierge acted like he could not be bothered to answer a question one morning However the desk clerk when we checked in was very pleasant There was not an elevator at the subway stop so it was difficult for me to get my luggage up the stairs upon arrival and down the stairs on departure   Location to the subway was down a back alley nighttime was unnerving Location to a Marks and Spencer grocery store and other shopping was nice Very good location to see Kensington Palace and gardens
The location of the hotel is great Near the underground and shops at High St Kensington The rooms are large and very clean Excellent value for money

We stayed in room 1133 and there was an issue with the electrics My husband switched the shower on and the bathroom light went off This concerned us and suggested that the electrics were problematic My husband did report the problem on two separate occasions Apart from that concern all was fine

Breakfast was cold especially the tea and coffee Should not have to pay extra for a hot chocolate and then receive a poor and cold one Should be served in a pot   Location was excellent and housekeeping service was quick Staff were lovely

Poor air conditioning and very noisy   Bed OK Cleaning could be better bath toilet very old need some renovation too many big furniture in the small room Some had spat in the hallway outside of room nr 323 it lay there for 4 days without being cleaned disgusting

Check in too ages and staff disappeared into separate office for every check in I watched We were only 15 mins before checkin time but flatly refused to check if our room was available Could clearly hear the conversation from the couple in the next room  Location

Hotel had no details of my booking Tried to move us to another hotel After two hours of talks finally obtained the four rooms booked I will not be using your booking service or this hotel in the future  Nothing

Noticed the glass protecting the furniture needed to be cleaned underneath coffee And sugar granules had gathered The towels rather thin and frayed discoloured And unable to extend the check out time did not have a flight out untl 10 pm so long wait Prior to the lengthy return journey  Beds comfortable however we were unable to alter the sound could not increase Love the tea and coffee facilities
Perhaps some biscuits with the coffee tea facilities   Convenient to underground shops Also walking distance to Kensington Gardens Good sized rooms with double bed

THE ROOM FO A START tHE TOWELS IN THE BATHROOM WERE SO THIN IT WAS DIFFICULT TO GET PROPARLY DRIED THE FRIDGE WAS EMPTY WHICH I FOUND UNBEILEVABLE THE HOTEL IS STUCK UN THE SEVENTIES AND IS NOT ACCEPTABLETO ME   Not Much The Reception was fine the room could benefit from a total upgade I didn t like it a all

This was a fabulous hotel my husband and I chose for our first wedding anniversary We were delighted to arrive and receive a complimentary upgrade and in our room we were overwhelmed to find a lovely card and cake to welcome and congraltualate us on our anniversary Could not reccomend this hotel enough so comfy and clean and great location

Rooms were extremely tired dated and the furniture was not in good condition at all Carpet was horrendous like being time transported to the 80 s But I hasten to add it was very clean and the bed although small was comfortable enough The windows were designed not to be opened so had to have a noisy air cooling system on that was very loud if you wanted the room cool enough for comfort The rooms were fine for us as we just needed a bed for the night whilst we were out shopping and site seeing in the day To be fair I wasn t expecting much anyway as it was the only hotel with rooms left after booking to see Adele who later cancelled her concert But if you wanted to treat yourself and your partner I d advise pick another hotel or upgrade the room   Breakfast was very good very good selection and tasty Staff were very friendly and efficient The loby and Resturant rooms were nice just a shame the bedrooms were not great Position was brilliant just a two minute walk to Kensington high street tube station on the circle and district lines and just two stops from paddington A couple of shops around that Close late boots etc and some cafes
Initially allocated an extremely poor room We complained immediately and we were moved We did have to pay an extra 60   Location and staff very helpful

Nothing  Lovely staff We were also lucky to be upgraded to guest of the day so had the added bonus of using the Executive Lounge If you can add this to your booking it is well worth it One of the most comfortable hotel beds I have slept in
It s London it was pricey But that s to be expected   The staff were without exception courteous and so helpful The breakfast was excellent Room clean quiet and comfortable

I booked 2 rooms my friends room was ready but I had to wait until nearly 3 30pmfor my room   Charles was exceptionally brilliant Gave us drinks vouchers for the bar as we had to leave before the free evening drinks I the Club Lounge

Overall brilliant but you could hear the train a little in the morning but I am a very light sleeper anyway The breakfast was 30 for 2 guests so I would suggest maybe eat out or book breakfast when booking the room   Comfy bed bathroom full of toiletry essentials hairdryer iron and fridge in room Got upgraded to a double room on arrival which was lovely No queues for the lift too

The rooms were very very dated Windows didn t open air conditioning poor Bathroom also very very dated and shower not powerful enough didn t like the old fashion rubber mat that was in the bath to stop slipping gem holder

we missed free drinks in club suite due to a dinner already booked ready for the concert NO other issues during the stay  We were the guests of the day So upgraded to club suite Fantastic room with excellent views of the city Breakfast was excellent all hot food was good and range was exceptional
The view from the room was not great it looked out to the back of the hotel I was happy with that as it was lovely and quiet and could not hear traffic noise
Hotel in need of refurbishment room worn down A lot of noise from underground track poor sound reduction in windows   Great location very close to underground
Breakfast is self service and we were later down the food was cold I would of complained on site but there were no staff around   The size of the bed and the room in general was great

The architecture is a shock I expected something grand given the location but Copthorne Tara is not grand Think functional and you ll be alright The windows didn t open The window unit was old and didn t work well which meant the room smelled musty and was too warm during a sunny summer visit Shower curtain smelled bad Could use some modern updates   Location is excellent Very close to High Street Kensington Station

Dont eat in the club lounge I found it to be quite small to cater for large mass of people at meal times Most staff are nice and accomodating but you do encounter a small number of staff that are rude and make sure you feel uncomfortable We ran out of toilet paper and it wasnt replaced during room service We rang concierge twice with no answer then attempted to contact reception which in turn transfered us to the same line again with no answer Please keep in mind I am not a whinger You just expect a standard of service for a facility that claims they are 4 stars   A lovely lady named Mai checked us in and upgraded us to a club room Location was great Train station just a short walk and so easy to use Restaurants and convenient store onsite incase you get so tired to go out for meals

In the up graded room the bed side lights witch did not work and so I had to turn the lights off by the door and find the bed in the dark   The bathroom was on the small side for an upgraded room I could not see what extras the upgrade I paid for had given me

Check in was a complete mess I had booked two club rooms and had previously spoke to reservation for two rooms adjacent one for me and one for my Daughter travelling from Toronto AND WAS ALLOCATED ROOMS 1269 AND 1271 ON THE 12TH FLOOR My daughter checked in and was allocated room 1269 Great I arrived an hour later and was told that the only room available was 1240 my room had been allocated to someone else I could not believe the incompetence After about 40 minutes they found two rooms together on the 11th floor So my Daughter had to move all her belongings from one room to another BREAKFAST being in a club room we had access to the club Lounge went for Breakfast could not get a table all occupied redirected to the Main breakfast room Took away the pleasure of eating in the CLUB lounge which we Paid for NOT IMPRESSED This Hotel certainly needs a manager  Staff were good

Crowds large groups everywhere tours small lobby area long quewes at reception area impossible to call reception during the busy day hours no one would answer staff mostly non British and not all understood the concept of good customer service very expensive food and drinks in the bar and restaurant restaurant set up for the tour groups and individual guests subject to large crowds and noise slow service stressed staff etc horrible and very scary old elevators harried front desk reception staff wish they would have told me about the small bar in the hotel off to the side that didn t cater to the large Tour groups overall lack of tranquility  Location is excellent

No bath robes in the room even after asking at reception Food was very expensive and not great   Comfortable beds nice breakfast in the club room which was advertised as continental but did actually include a cooked breakfast

Service in hotel was extremely slow Walked out of Asian restaurant as sat for so long without even getting a drink offered The tea at breakfast was disgusting 32 for 4 glasses of wine is daylight robbery   Location was brilliant Very close to shops bars restaurants sight seeing and Tube 2 mins from hotel

nearly 30 minutes to check in check in clerk was not aware of Artists Corner restaurant for which I had requested a reservation online and said it was not at that hotel Lunchtime service in the brasserie on 29th was very poor and two of the five dishes served were disappointing   upgraded room because of poor service at reception was a bonus Nice room and comfortable bed

Everything was positive to us Grand daughter me Loved it  Some times you find a wonderful unexpected experience This was one Loved everything about this hotel Been telling everyone about this wonderful find Large rooms Executive lounge All pluses
They weren t particularly helpful when I tried to arrange an early check in  Nice gesture in providing us with some cake to celebrate my 70th birthday

Late notice of move more expensive journeys to and from the event I was attending I booked into the Tara and was told that it was nearer to where my dinner was being held itn wasn t and I was very late The staff at the Copthorne Fulham were fantastic but the comms between the two hotels was poor   Great reception staff at the Copthorne in Fulham I was moved from the Tara to the Fulham one at late notice it cost me extra on the tube as this was in Zone 2

Our only quibbles were that the room was very hot we were at the back end of a heatwave in London and the air conditioning was very very noisy and the windows did not open at all Also strangely the supplies of toiletries provided did not include a shower gel or hand soap   Staff were very helfpful and friendly Room was spacious and comfortable We did not eat in the hotel as we preferred to use the numerous cafes and restaurants very close by in Kensington High Street but did have a drink in the bar which was very pleasant
Room very stuffy with no access to fresh air Bed had very firm mattress which affected my back   The location was excellent the staff extremely helpful and friendly Good free internet access

I ve stayed here a lot over the years which makes it feel a bit home from home for me however the rooms are getting very tired now and bathrooms really aren t up to par Staff always amazing and that s what keeps me coming back however Copthorne please please re vitalise your rooms   Staff here are always friendly and helpful Location is good too

Many facilities shop etc were shut when I got back from a concert at 10pm This is not late if returning from a show so was disappointing I had booked an accessible room but it was difficult to find my way around I could have phoned reception for advice but it shouldn t have needed that Had to get out of bed to switch off the light as was intimidated by all the alarm buttons and couldn t find the bedside light switch for fear of summoning help by accident Better instructions to the room would help or someone should automatically accompany guests and talk them through everything It was a very small single bed It might be different if travelling with a companion as there was an adjoining door Do not know what the adjoining room was like   Breakfast was excellent Staff were delightful

The only thing we would have liked was a vending machine with some chocolate as we were craving some late at night and there wasn t any in the hotel haha   The staff were extremely friendly and welcoming Check in wasn t a problem arrived there round 4pm and there were only a couple of people in front of us The bed was comfy shower was great and the air con worked perfectly Had reservations on it from reading previous reviews but we stayed in London on one of the hottest days of the year and it kept the room at a good temp

a lift or disability access from the car park to reception would be good   The friendliness and general demeanour of all staff They were exemplary and made us welcome from the first meeting registering till when we left
Bathroom door did not shut properly Ran out of tissues and toilet paper   Delicious Eggs Benedict Royal cooked perfectly Excellent customer service All staff very polite

Excellent location Great hotel for business stays or tourists as Kensington Palace and Royal Albert Hall are not far away The skyline view from the 12th Floor and executive breakfast made the stay particularly enjoyable I would stay again
Fridge in room was a bit noisy and could not locate a switch to turn off as we were not using it but this is a minor fault   Nice comfortable room No noise from outside
Rooms a bit tired a c very poor not particularly efficient and very noisy  Staff and general facilities seemed very good only there one night so don t have a particularly informed view
Nothing to dislike  Very clean Comfortable beds Very quiet Very close to Kensington high street Lovely bars loads of very good places to buy good food Good base for touring London

Our room had an awful smell of dampness I did report it the carepet was soaking wet from the air on I think that is where the smell came from I had to spray the room with perfume and deodorant to try and help get rid of the smell it made me feel quite sick   I like it as it was a buffet very good food

This was my second business trip to this hotel in one month I stayed a total of 10 days on two business trips in the Club Room level of the Hotel and I will not be going back The first night at the hotel was a disaster The noise was so bad from the Air conditioning units outside that I had to change my room three times To give the hotel some credit they did try hard to resolve the problem but the issue is the hotel itself rather than the personnel working there This hotel needs an upgrade especially the Double Glazing There is absolutely no soundproofing at this hotel and all the noise from outside can be heard in the room I was there with two other work colleagues who all complained of the same issue One of my colleagues who stayed on the other side of the Hotel said that he could hear the Train literally passing in Stereo through his room all night We all went to our conferences very tired and agitated The crazy thing about this experience was that we stayed in the Club Room level of the Hotel These were supposed to be some of the best rooms in the Hotel The other thing I noticed was the beds do not have fitted Bed sheets covering the matress They just use Flat sheets which means that in the morning you are often lying on top of the mattress with half a sheet under you This cannot be hyginic and is not something that I have seen in other hotels The bed was also very hard and uncomfortable Certainly not what you would expect from this standard of Hotel As part of my job I spend at least 2 weeks of every month travelling around Europe and unfortunately I have to give this hotel 4 10   The best thing about this hotel is the location near to High street Kensington But do not expect to sleep well at this hotel as there is absolutely no soundproofing and beds are hard and uncomfortable

Noise of trains was terrible up to 2 am and started again at 5 30am It was the hottest week in 20 years but it was really uncomfortable as windows did not open and the air con did not work that well and very noisy when on so had to turn off   Nice staff
Breakfast was extra in the Exec Lounge and was paid for it was poor slow service and bad value for money it was over subscribed so table space was limited

The power sockets for charging phones or batteries were located in awkward places like corner of the room a few centimeters from the ground Better if they were located at site and height of bedside table of writing table   i liked being just a 5 to 7 minute walk to the underground the rooms were spacious clean and comfortable the staff from doorman to front desk were all polite and helpful

the lounge was very busy at the time we went for breakfast we must have chosen a popular time It was difficult to get a table and the staff seemed overstretched or inadequately trained They were all very frienfly and courteous but it seemed rather chaotic for a short while The cutlery given was inadequate fork knife and spoon I would have liked a side knife to spread the butter etc a fork to use with my spoon for the fresh fruit and a teaspoon That caused unnecessary delay and duplication of work for the waiters as we waited for them to be brought from the kitchen   We paid extra to use the executive lounge and this was well worth the extra cost The small shop at the entrance was invaluable we bought tasty fresh sandwiches for our journey home
Breakfast no up to much If staying here again wouldn t bother with it Plenty of nice little cafes up the road  Great location very near to tube station room basic but clean and quiet

No hairdryer I asked the maid in my area She did not speak English had no idea what I was asking Since my hair was wet there was no point in calling for a dryer as my hair would have been dry before the dryer arrived   The location is good Tube station buses just round the corner

I suppose the room could have been a bit bigger but arriving from a number of days in France the room seemed big enough   This was our second visit to The Copthorne Tara We stayed in a Club Room which is on the top floors of the hotel The room included a very good continental breakfast with hot choices such as scrambled eggs and sausage served nicely in the Club Lounge Also included was access to wine and beer for an hour and a half in the evening served in the same lounge The bed was very comfortable and the room had a nicely appointed bathroom

the hotel is badly needing up graded the furniture was very worn the pillows were as flat as pancakes the bedding should be binned new quilt covers are not expensive the food at breakfast was not hot   The only thing was the convenience to underground

The room was not as depicted in the photographs The TV was malfunctioning The Commode had stains the hand shower was jerky The purse of two of our friends was stolen from the restaurant during breakfast time   The staff were excellent

Have stayed before On this occasion the room was awful Grubby carpets Torn bed surrounds Raw plugs in several places where items removed Tv not working Spoke to reception Hotel very busy Only there one night and too late to move on

The hotel public areas are OK but the room they originally put me in was grim room 38 on the mezzanine in case you need to avoid I had booked myself into a double room and inadvertently to one where mobility could be assisted I found myself in a single room at the far end of the corridor on the mezzanine which looked like it hadn t been refurbished in decades and which reminded me of a run down hospital with bed and bath lifts The windows didn t open and there was a stale smell the chair had a stain which I feared was urine I really could believe they were charging central London four star prices for this and felt people with mobility difficulties would get a terribly raw deal They did transfer me to a double room that was merely shabby rather than grim I have made a mental note to never return here

I could hear the neighbours through the wall which wasn t too bad as they didn t come back late and didn t have the TV on but if they had it would have been noisy   The bed was very comfortable and the room a decent size It was in a nice quiet location and I could hear birds singing in the morning It was a lovely walk across Hyde Park to where I work

Toilet is very loud as it is being refilled with water Airco is ancient and very loud but it works   Room was quiet enough check in was fast You could switch off the fridge Chromecast was avalaible for the tv so that we could watch Netflix in the evenings
Pillows are too soft   Room and bathroom size are good Near highstreet kensington station with Marks and Spencer and Boots Quite a number of cafes There s Madonald s too

Service was a little slow around reception and checking in otherwise all was fine  The location and underground car parking make this a best choice for travellers by car wishing to stay in central London
Nothing   All was lovely I would recommend this property to anyone who wants reasonable accommodation and food close to the tube at a very reasonable price

Upgrade to superior room with breakfast breakfast good but room far from superior not worth extra money I suspect room would of been the same My advice ask to view both rooms then decide   Great location

Room we were given was in the lower ground and was not 4 star standard It was very dark and claustrophobic also the bed was very small I doubt it was a full size double As we were strapped for time I did not request another room We have stayed at this hotel a couple of times and have been happy with the rooms which is why I was so disappointed this time  The breakfast was good value

I rang the hotel direct to try and book parking and was told to book through Justpark com However this cost 15 extra which I assumed would be refunded at the hotel as they advertised 25 parking fee This was not the case staff saying they didn t know anything about it I was very unhappy but the staff tried to be helpful and gave us free breakfasts to make up for the overpayment we d incurred In the future though all hotel staff need to give the same correct information   Very helpful staff comfy bed and tasty breakfast

Tipping at 10   Excellent hotel with very helpful staff and easy check in out Room was very comfortable although we had a problem with the room key that had to be replaced several times Good breakfast everyday and extra places to eat dinner

nothing  good location given a room upgrade on arrival which was unexpected and very nice room very good size with good clean facilities good breakfast plenty of choice and nice restaurant pleasant and efficient staff

the only thing that let the place down was the fact that our room also overlooked high street kensington tube station so noise from trains coming and going was at times a little annoying especially early inn the morning and late at night   Very clean Well run We upgraded to a club room on arrival so can t compare to what we would have had But the added benefit of breakfast and drinks with canapes made it well worth the extra cost The room had a great view of the London skyline So all in all a thoroughly enjoyable stay

Lifts Busy at breakfast and didn t always stop on the floor you wanted ended up going up and down a few times before getting to floor 6  Over all the hotel was very good Breakfast was lovely and lots of choice Would have liked porridge It wasn t on offer but not the end of the world

Poor communications skills and lack of curtesy of restaurant staff  Proximity to major attractions Quick access to the train network Happy Hour after a big day out Bistro Public Bar General friendliness helpfullness of front of house staff

No complimentary shower gel in bathroom or walk in shower Room service menu limited and expensive if required after 10 pm Food in bar restaurant average Though plenty of choice close by In hindsight I would have waited to book until much closer the time as room prices drop dramatically on line and breakfast is also included in deals Anyone booking two days before my visit was paying 60 less than me and had breakfast included A little annoying considering I honoured my booking reservation   Bright clean room Good air conditioning Good choice of 3 on site restaurants or other local well known eating places Wide choice for breakfast Friendly helpful staff 2 mins walk to Kensington High Street and tube Easy 20 min walk to museums and 15 min to Royal Albert Hall Hyde park minutes away so handy for lovely walk to Serpentine etc

Despite requesting an extra bed with booking com AND calling the property direct to ensure ok AND confirming and paying for this at check in there was still no extra bed The room was small and the sink blocked Also a bit noisy as put in a room on a railway track Room was also very stuffy had to go into corridors for air   Food available late and fantastic choice for breakfast

No plug in the bath so I had to take a shower water wasn t very hot Room could have done with a lick of paint as it was starting to look tired   Staff were very friendly during check in and out Breakfast was excellent and the waiting staff were always asking if I wanted more coffee Chef overheard me asking for Hash browns and personally went off to get me some

I could have put excellent but as i have booked 6 nights stay i was given a room on he 2nd floor next to he station so you can imagine as if i was sleeping in the station itself Every 5 10 mins i was awaken by the train arrival at the station i lost almost all day next waiting to be given another room finally i had a room at about 2 30 with 2 SINGLES bed on the 6th floor  I have REALLY Appreciated the early check in thank You so much The Staff at the lounge Club EXCELLENT Especially there is a lady that i would like to send a special thoughts she works in the evening

I waited for about 10 minutes in the bar bistro for anyone to acknowledge me and bring a food menu Eventually I went to get this myself After another 5 minutes I still had nobody attend to me so I left to eat elsewhere  Large clean room with good heating cooling system

The bedrooms and bathroom s are looking tired based on modern hotel standards but the price for a central London hotel reflects this The hotel is very good value for it s location   Very good stay as usual

hearing people talking etc in the room next door they did not answer their wake up call until the third attempt to contact them and we had to say answer your phone in our room before they did Although we were out of the room by 9 and did not return till 4 it had not been cleaned more than enough time for this to have occurred The white wine in the bar is mingling   It was within reasonable walking distance from the tube station Very nice Thai restaurant round the corner

Firstly the car park has tiny spaces and difficult to manoeuvre around with many cars parking across two spaces do they pay double I wonder There is no lift from the car park to reception so you walk down a dingy corridor with hanging pipes and cables through doors with chipped paint and up about 20 steps struggling with luggage to reception Here you wait in a queue for about ten minutes to check in because guess what only three of the six check in desks are manned and a coach load of tourists has just beaten you to check in We ask a bored looking concierge if there is somewhere for our 82 year old disabled mother to sit whilst we get our key and are directed to a few stools in an area cordoned off by a red rope which he makes no attempt to move for us so we can gain access so we move it ourselves We finally check in but only after having to give again exactly the same information that we had given when making the original booking We were then asked what time we were checking out on our leaving day Not soon enough I was tempted to reply and we had only been here about half an hour We were due to go to a show at the Albert Hall so back to our friendly concierge once again but he said he didn t know where the Albert Hall was but asked an equally bored looking colleague who muttered it would be five minutes by cab My faith would have been lifted a little if he has said brightly Let me know what time you wish to leave sir and I will hail a cab for your party but I suppose we can only dream We had booked two rooms together so we re not really surprised when one was on the first floor and the other on the fifth The rooms were clean but basic with ultra thin pillows no choice of pillow like Premier Inn and the narrowest double bed I have seen in a hotel for a good many years now and we have been to quite a few We decided to eat at the Brasserie before going to our show   Nothing much apart from the location

Reception staff were not efficient concierge sullen When asked a question I don t know is not an acceptable answer Pillows were lumpy   Location

This is a huge hotel and there are many tour groups staying The reception and guest service desk concierge are understaffed I had to wait for 10 minutes to check in and another 10 minutes to collect my suitcase I left at the guest service although there was no queue When a large group of guests arrives waiting time will be extraordinary They charged 40 in addition to the room charge on my credit card without telling me I know some hotels request pre authorisation for a certain amount to cover additional charges during the stay but normally they tell me before doing so Charging customers credit card without notifying them is not a good idea They didn t give me a invoice receipt at the check out I assumed they would email me as some of the hotels recently do again they normally ask if I want a receipt emailed or in paper However I was later on told when I called the hotel to enquire about the unknown credit card charge that they wouldn t email a receipt unless I specifically request it That means they wouldn t give you a receipt unless you ask which is unusual and rude Their complementary wifi was slow and you will possibly have to pay for a faster connection if you would like to do some work or watch online video The room didn t have an available socket around the desk or bed so I had to work on my laptop on the TV stand which was inconvenient   Good location 3 minute walk from the High Street Kensington station The bathroom had a bathtub It was clean and the shower had a good pressure of water Free wifi

Inability to move the chairs into a position where it was comfortable for each of us to watch TV Noise from underground was disturbing at times   The view from floor 12 was excellent We enjoyed the food both at breakfast and in the evening The shower was good but would have preferred a walk in shower rather than over the bath The staff were extremely helpful

No negative points  The room 644 was excellent and all the staff were very good I left some purchased goods in a drawer in the room and the follow up has been first class goods now in the lost property department Kevin has been most helpful as have housekeeping and will be collected by a friend in the next few days I am most grateful to all concerned

Nothing Seriously i have thought for few minutes still nothing   Everything The entrance was extra grand Parking is extra close by The staff was extra friendly and super helpful The view in the club rooms is extra nice The bed linen is super comfy the pillows I could keep on and on The bacon is extra good The Breakfast in club lounge is worth coming here and made me think the room comes as a free extra to it so deserves an extra merit Unlike many nowadays this Hotel is not understaffed and even at rush hour times they coped with our large group and exceeded my not so humble expectations I have stayed in other 4 star hotels in London this one is number one and is worth every penny The glitch with the wifi was solved in minutes and only revealed how quickly the Hotel handles any problems Do not hesitate a real 10
Short curtains meant sunlight flooded into the room right at eye level from 5 00am Also the bed was soft and bouncy making it difficult not to disturb each other   Handy for Kensington High Street

Scrambled egg was hey runny on two occasions in the executive lounge It was inedible Some train noise but it didn t keep me awake Fridge in room not working on arrival but fixed very quickly after we reported it Overall a great stay We would book again   Very friendly staff and a great location Very clean public areas and great breakfast Paid to upgrade to have breakfast which included a club room with great views Air con was a blessing as was the fridge for our drinks

We were originally given a room on the 1st floor at the end of a corridor It was noisy sounded like construction work next door The shower didn t work didn t switch from bath to shower We were changed to a room on the 8th floor I would be upset if I d paid more for this room as it was very similar to the one on the 1st floor In summary very disappointed with the stay The lady on reception didn t seem to appreciate why I was so upset and very reluctantly went to ask a manager for help It was a special treat weekend for us but it was spoiled by our experience at the Copthorne Tara   Location parking good

The bed was very hard and the door was not sound proof at all you could hear everything going on in the hall The fridge in the room made such a racket that we had to switch it off to have any chance of sleep The noise from the Underground that passes right by the hotel was also a bit annoying The room was superficially clean but we found hairs in the bath shower when we arrived and when we wetted a flannel and wiped the bathroom door and handle quite a bit of dirt came off   Upgraded to a club room which included a breakfast that was excellent The location was good but unfortunately High Street Kensington station was closed that weekend so it was a long walk to the nearest Underground

District Circle lines didn t have service between Earl s Court and Edgware Road So I had to take a bus to High St Kensington When I left the hotel I walked to Gloucester Road   A very clean room a very comfortable bed good wifi connection nothing to complain The reception staff was very nice A nice Thai restaurant and a nice muffin breakfast place are just beside the hotel

We had one of the rooms that wasn t renovated I thought it was okay though a little dated but my sister found it extremely ugly The first day my towel smelled like it had been left wet too long It was better on the second day so hopefully that isn t a regular thing Everything was clean otherwise   The beds were very comfortable The staff was friendly The place was quite clean They had a full array of bath products much better than the very common all in one wall mounted dispenser a personal pet peeve of mine as a woman traveller It was only a few minutes walk from the Tube and bus stops and there was a very nice little chain cafe called Nero on the way to the Tube where we ate breakfast twice The Muffin Man Teahouse was also good but I m not sure I would recommend it I had the feeling that the staff wasn t treated well by the owner There was also plenty of shopping just across the street from the nearest Tube Check out was very quick If your tab is taken care of all you have to do is drop your keys in the bowl on the counter

Very dirty and dated room An extra bed required the removal of most of the furniture from the room leaving us with nowhere to sit Had we known about this we would have paid more and booked a different hotel I even telephone before I booked to make sure a third bed was no problem and to explain the importance of this The extra bed did not even fit in the room after the removal of furniture and left nowhere to walk safely Slightly larger rooms are available on the top floor but one of these was not used There were no dressing gowns or slippers as advertised the shower was broken etc etc etc I could go on The only good thing was that there was a phone and so we could call down for the repairman The hotel just wasn t up to scratch I travel a lot for work and I have stayed in far far better Premier and Jury s Inns It spoilt what was supposed to be and was otherwise a very special treat for a young boy s birthday as well as his first stay in London We were told that a gift would be left in the room which I thought was a nice touch but this never materialised The Club Room which we paid extra for was too full for our breakfast and so we were made to join the end of a very long queue in the main lounge until we complained When I asked about these things the receptionist pretended to go a speak to a manager but I believe this was a way of getting me to go away Never again and I shall think very carefully when I book any other hotel in London if I bother at all If anyone does book go with very very low expectations   The staff were excellent

We did not like the waiter at the restaurant who served us dinner on December 30 2015 evening He did not change the table cloth which had food stains looked unhygienic When pointed out he just scratched on the stain blew some air from his mouth and left like that He was not courteous and his etiquette was not befitting his role as a waiter of a decent restaurant Our opinion is that this person was not of Austrian origin he has not received proper training he does not know does not care about the values and customs of Austria Western Europe It is good that immigrants are given jobs but this cannot happen at the cost of not ensuring that they are fit for the job and without adequate and proper training It should be emphasized that the person who attended to us during our breakfasts was just the opposite He was excellent courteous friendly with a smile and service minded He spoke German and English too and was of European origin We are not prejudiced so we hope our criticism and remarks will be understood and taken in a positive way The room It was too hot and thus very uncomfortable and difficult to sleep at night The air temperature control did not function This is a waste of energy which one should be concerned about in the context of climate change problems Furthermore think of the energy savings that can be achieved Courtyard by Marriot must do something in this respect   Location close to the Metro station Ramp at the entrance facilitates transport of luggage very much appreciated The room cleaning personnel Housekeeping were very service minded and do deserve praise and appreciation

We asked for double bed but we got a twin room instead without any explanation or excuse The elevator is slow the parking is 19 eur per day and the garage is very complicated to use The room is not very clean for example there was mold on a shower holder and stains on the bathroom door I had some problems with internet in the room as well even though you mostly pay extra for it   The location is good for Vienna near Schoenbrunn and near metro station 6 stops to the city centre The beds were comfortable and the room was spacious

We usually go to small family run hotels or bed and breakfasts but thought we would treat ourselves to something upmarket and go to a Marriott This hotel was not at all what we were expecting It was more like a UK Travel Lodge or Premiere Inn where we have had great stays it is just that this was not what we were expecting Staff were generally somewhat grumpy though we understand this is not unusual in Vienna Our bed was broken and could not be used There was no alternative room for us and no manager available to speak to As the next day was a Saturday we were told there would still be no manager to speak to We were offered camp beds We were understandably unhappy about this so the bed was fixed late in the evening by putting boxes underneath it This worked and the staff dealing with this were very friendly but again it is not what should have been expected Internet was only free in public areas and did not work well there In general we were very disappointed after looking forward so much to our visit to this hotel We visit Austria fairly often but will not go back to this hotel There are plenty others to choose from   The location was good for our needs as we were visiting family nearby It was also good for the Schoenbrunn Christmas Market and the underground for getting into the centre of Vienna There were tea and coffee making facilities in the room which we have not been used to in Austria which was good and the room was very clean and quiet

The air con is not very responsive in our case anyway We could not get the temperature to drop below 22 7   The breakfast is excellent with a wide variety of foods The staff were always close by to assist should you need it We ate in the main restaurant one evening and we both thought the food was outstanding with a price that is in our opinion very reasonable located about 20 mins South of Amsterdam City the Metro 15 mins walk runs every 10 minutes service 54 The hotel provide a shuttle should you need it i e torrential rain A bargain at 3 each The local area should you like to explore has a large lake opposite where you can walk around and take in some of the local countryside a lovely spot with loads of waterfowl and other wildlife Overall we rate this hotel 8 5 out of ten and wouldn t hesitate to stay there again
